presenting_org: FCC Office of Engineering and Technology

slides.title: Study Objectives and Scope
bullets: Evaluate interference potential from 5G NR into federal radar systems in the 3.5 GHz band
Assess coexistence feasibility under ITU-R propagation models
Determine minimum frequency separation and guard band requirements
slides.notes: Opening slide — provides context for the full analysis.
slides.raw_text:
Study Objectives and Scope
Evaluate interference potential from 5G NR into federal radar systems in the 3.5 GHz band
Assess coexistence feasibility under ITU-R propagation models
Determine minimum frequency separation and guard band requirements

slides.title: Analytical Assumptions
bullets: 5G NR EIRP: 46 dBm per sector, per 3GPP TS 38.104
OOBE level: -13 dBm/MHz at band edge
Propagation model: ITM (Longley-Rice) for terrain-sensitive paths
Receiver threshold: -107 dBm for radar protection criterion
Deployment density: 4 base stations per km² in suburban areas
Guard band: 10 MHz assumed between licensed allocations
slides.notes: These assumptions are preliminary and subject to agency review.
slides.raw_text:
Analytical Assumptions
5G NR EIRP: 46 dBm per sector, per 3GPP TS 38.104
OOBE level: -13 dBm/MHz at band edge
Propagation model: ITM (Longley-Rice) for terrain-sensitive paths
Receiver threshold: -107 dBm for radar protection criterion
Deployment density: 4 base stations per km² in suburban areas
Guard band: 10 MHz assumed between licensed allocations

slides.title: Interference Analysis Results
bullets: 5G NR base stations may interfere with federal radar receivers within 5 km
Aggregate interference exceeds the -107 dBm threshold under dense deployment
Guard band of 10 MHz reduces interference by 18 dB
Coexistence is manageable with coordination zones and power control
slides.notes: Preliminary findings — simulation runs still in progress.
slides.raw_text:
Interference Analysis Results
5G NR base stations may interfere with federal radar receivers within 5 km
Aggregate interference exceeds the -107 dBm threshold under dense deployment
Guard band of 10 MHz reduces interference by 18 dB
Coexistence is manageable with coordination zones and power control

slides.title: Path Loss vs. Distance (Figure 3)
slides.notes: Figure 3 — path loss curves for ITM model at 3.5 GHz over flat and hilly terrain.
slides.raw_text:

slides.title: Open Issues and Questions for Agency Review
bullets: What is the DoD radar receiver threshold under worst-case multipath conditions?
Are coordination zone boundaries aligned with existing federal frequency assignments?
TBD: clutter model for coastal/maritime deployment scenarios
slides.notes: Items require resolution before the working paper advances.
